articles.title: Hey, Siri: Apple just announced a long-awaited AI update
articles.description: At its annual developers' conference, Apple put the spotlight on new AI features, while highlighting security and child safety ‚ÄĒ and critiquing the company's AI competitors.

articles.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store

articles.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
articles.description: Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At WWDC 2026, the company announced a host of new AI-powered photo editing tools.
articles.content: Apple‚Äôs feature showcase at WWDC 2026 didn‚Äôt flag which if these ‚Äúphotographs‚ÄĚ are real or created with its new AI fakery. | Images by Apple / compiled by The Verge Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At WWDC 2026 , the company announced a host of new AI-powered photo editing tools . They give users effortless power
articles.author: Jess Weatherbed

articles.title: Home sales surge to the fastest pace this year despite rising mortgage rates, prices
articles.description: Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes accelerated last month to their fastest pace since December, a sharp turnaround in demand for the housing market after a lackluster start to the spring homebuying season

articles.title: Gold isn‚Äôt inert, it just has bodyguards protecting it
articles.description: Individual gold atoms move around to form oxidation-proof structures.
articles.content: Gold is weird. It's one of the few metals that doesn‚Äôt really oxidize. Even silver and copper‚ÄĒfrom the same column of the periodic table‚ÄĒform weak oxides. Naively, you might expect that gold would tarnish just like silver. Gold also sits right next to platinum, but it has none of that metal‚Äôs catalytic properties. Then came gold nanoparticles that acted like catalysts, and we were confused by their apparent willingness to take part in chemical reactions. Now, a pair of scientists has explained t
articles.author: Chris Lee

articles.title: David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in the XPrize competition
articles.description: The outspoken longevity scientist David Sinclair has been predicting that one day, you‚Äôll go to the doctor and get a prescription that will make you 10 years younger. Now MIT Technology Review has learned that he has plans to launch human tests of an oral “reprogramming” drug as part of
articles.content: The outspoken longevity scientist David Sinclair has been predicting that one day, you‚Äôll go to the doctor and get a prescription that will make you 10 years younger. Now MIT Technology Review has learned that he has plans to launch human tests of an oral “reprogramming” drug as part of a $101 million competition organized by the XPrize Foundation. The foundation is offering cash awards to teams able to ‚Äúrestore‚ÄĚ a person to an earlier apparent age, as measured by improvements in imm
articles.author: Antonio Regalado

articles.title: EU orders Meta to open WhatsApp to rival AI chatbots
articles.description: Meta has accused the EU of "regulatory overreach" and says its decision will allow tech giants like OpenAI free access to WhatsApp.
articles.content: Meta has accused the EU of "regulatory overreach" and says its decision will allow tech giants like OpenAI free access to WhatsApp.
articles.author: BBC Technology

articles.title: It‚Äôs not FAANG anymore. It‚Äôs MANGOS.
articles.description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords ‚ÄĒ and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.
articles.content: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords ‚ÄĒ and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

articles.title: Learning to lead in a hybrid human-AI enterprise
articles.description: As adoption of AI agents looks set to surge by as much as 300% in the next two years, leadership teams are carefully considering the implications of a hybrid human-AI workforce. Unlike existing enterprise-level automation that relies on manual input, AI agents are capable of autonomously coordinatin
articles.content: As adoption of AI agents looks set to surge by as much as 300% in the next two years, leadership teams are carefully considering the implications of a hybrid human-AI workforce. Unlike existing enterprise-level automation that relies on manual input, AI agents are capable of autonomously coordinating complex tasks, interacting with multiple tools and environments across an organization. In early applications that center on customer service, HR, and sales, adoption of agentic AI has led to produc
articles.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

articles.title: With just days left, the U.S. opening match at the World Cup is still not sold out
articles.description: Hundreds of tickets are still available for the U.S. and Canada opening matches for the World Cup on Friday. Even more are available in resale platforms ‚ÄĒ many at below face value.
articles.content: Hundreds of tickets are still available for the U.S. and Canada opening matches for the World Cup on Friday. Even more are available in resale platforms ‚ÄĒ many at below face value. (Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)

articles.title: Longevity Startup Doses First Human in Bid to Reverse Age-Related Sight Loss
articles.description: The FDA recently approved the cellular rejuvenation therapy ER-100 for human clinical trials. While vision is the first target, it could have applications for a variety of age-related disease.
articles.content: The FDA recently approved the cellular rejuvenation therapy ER-100 for human clinical trials. While vision is the first target, it could have applications for a variety of age-related disease.

articles.title: Building a stateless process supervisor in Go: what the OS fights you on
articles.description: Most "run my stack locally" setups converge on one of: a pile of terminal tabs and shell history, Docker Compose (great when you want containers, heavy when you want a binary and a log file), a real supervisor ( supervisord , systemd user units) ‚ÄĒ powerful, but you are signing up for install surface
articles.content: Most "run my stack locally" setups converge on one of: a pile of terminal tabs and shell history, Docker Compose (great when you want containers, heavy when you want a binary and a log file), a real supervisor ( supervisord , systemd user units) ‚ÄĒ powerful, but you are signing up for install surface, config dialects, and often a long-lived daemon. I wanted something narrower: declare processes in YAML , start them in the background , know if they are alive , stop them predictably , tail logs , a
articles.author: Yussuf Ajao

articles.title: "A no-key web search API for AI agents, and the MCP server that wraps it"
articles.description: I have been building tooling for AI agents in Python for about a year. The thing I keep needing, over and over, is "give the agent a search bar." Every time, the search bar costs me an account, an API key, a billing relationship, and a way to keep that key out of the repo. The first three are fricti
articles.content: I have been building tooling for AI agents in Python for about a year. The thing I keep needing, over and over, is "give the agent a search bar." Every time, the search bar costs me an account, an API key, a billing relationship, and a way to keep that key out of the repo. The first three are friction; the fourth is risk. A few weeks ago I came across a public endpoint that does not have any of those: GET https://scouts-ai.com/api/search . No header for auth, no signup, no rate-limit-agreement l
articles.author: Aloya

articles.title: Building a Context-Aware Onboarding System in Next.js
articles.description: When I started building TourKit, I thought onboarding tours would be the easy part. Create a few tooltips, highlight some elements, and move users from step to step. Turns out I was completely wrong. The real challenge wasn't building the UI. It was deciding when a tour should start. The Problem wit
articles.content: When I started building TourKit, I thought onboarding tours would be the easy part. Create a few tooltips, highlight some elements, and move users from step to step. Turns out I was completely wrong. The real challenge wasn't building the UI. It was deciding when a tour should start. The Problem with Traditional Tours Most onboarding systems follow a fixed flow: Dashboard ‚Üí Analytics ‚Üí Settings ‚Üí Complete But real users don't behave like that. Some users land directly on the analytics page. Othe
articles.author: Raj Chavan

articles.title: US military claims first drone boat rescue of downed helicopter crew
articles.description: US Navy‚Äôs Task Force 59 achieved the drone rescue at sea near Strait of Hormuz.
articles.content: A drone boat picked up two US Army pilots from waters near the Strait of Hormuz after their helicopter gunship went down, US military officials said in interviews with various broadcast news outlets. The incident apparently represents the first time the US military has used a drone for such a rescue mission at sea. The two crew members from the US Army AH-64 Apache were ‚Äúrescued by American forces‚ÄĚ at 7:33 pm US Eastern Time after their helicopter went down off the coast of Oman on June 8, accor

articles.content: A different kind of Sora is back in the news. Square Enix just teased the long-awaited next entry in the Kingdom Hearts series, though there's not much in the way of info just yet. It's called Kingdom Hearts IV and it's launching on the Switch 2, PS5, Xbox, and PC, but there's no release date in sight. It's the first all-new entry in the series since KH III in 2019 . You can watch the brief new teaser trailer above. That's not the only KH -related news from the Nintendo Direct. If you missed out

articles.description: Nintendo announced a remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time during its Nintendo Direct presentation on Tuesday. It will launch sometime in 2026 on Nintendo Switch 2. More details will be announced "in the future." This new title is the first major release in the franchise since 2024's Echoes
articles.content: Nintendo announced a remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time during its Nintendo Direct presentation on Tuesday. It will launch sometime in 2026 on Nintendo Switch 2 . More details will be announced "in the future." This new title is the first major release in the franchise since 2024's Echoes of Wisdom , which starred Princess Zelda herself. In a blog post on Tuesday, Meta explains that it already uses your off-platform activity, like the games you play or your purchases on other websites, to serve you ads. But now it's expanding t
articles.content: Meta is planning to use the data shared by other businesses to personalize your feed and its AI responses. In a blog post on Tuesday , Meta explains that it already uses your off-platform activity, like the games you play or your purchases on other websites, to serve you ads. But now it's expanding the scope of the content it personalizes across Facebook and Instagram. For example, Meta says if you bought a tent online recently, you might see camping-related videos in your Reels feed. "We aren't

articles.title: From one-off prompts to workflows: How to use custom agents in GitHub Copilot CLI
articles.description: Custom agents let GitHub Copilot CLI understand your stack and team workflows, turning one-off terminal prompts into repeatable, reviewable processes. The post From one-off prompts to workflows: How to use custom agents in GitHub Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: Developers work across many surfaces like the CLI, IDE, and GitHub. The terminal is often where they turn to move fast, automate tasks, or work directly with systems and scripts. Tools like the GitHub Copilot CLI already make this easier. You can generate commands, debug issues, and move quicker without leaving the terminal. However, like any environment, the CLI can still accumulate friction: re-running the same commands, re-explaining context, or translating logs for your team into something t
articles.author: Natalie Guevara

articles.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single errant character
articles.description: Use-after-free bug can be exploited to evade sandbox defenses.
articles.content: Researchers have analyzed a high-severity vulnerability in Linux that‚Äôs able to escalate untrusted users to root by exploiting a bug you don't often see: a single errant character inside the kernel. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-23111 , is located in nf_tables, a subsystem of the Linux kernel that provides packet filtering capabilities. It‚Äôs used to manage firewall rules and replaces older subsystems such as iptables, ip6tables, arptables, and ebtables. !!!WTF!!! The presence of a singl
articles.author: Dan Goodin

articles.content: Welcome back to GitHub for Beginners. This is the final episode of the season, and we’ve covered a lot so far. Make sure to check out our other episodes to see all the various topics we’ve discussed. Today, we’re going to spend some time answering some questions that people often have, especially when they’re first getting started. So without further ado, let’s jump right in.
